title: Prelude is packed u16 target/control root-link fabric
evidence: Large population of prelude pairs target >=0x1760 and land on marker-rich subtrees; clusters align with Stage 58 low-offset hotspots.
implication: Parser should treat prelude as auxiliary link table, not arbitrary numeric noise.

title: Early FMV nodes are directly reachable through root-link targets
evidence: Exact or near-exact prelude hits for FUJ3, GO2, MICROIDS, CHALL02, INV1, ELF3 within <=0x80 bytes of their node/string neighborhoods.
implication: FMV addressing bridge can be prototyped first on early PSS entries.

title: Late FMV nodes require second-level bank/page decoding
evidence: No comparable direct u16 bridge for FMV entries beyond 0xFFFF even though clear node records exist.
implication: Need Stage 60 to decode bank/page or second-level indirection for late FMV assets.

title: Control halfword encodes structural edge/class state
evidence: Recurring control values fan into semantically coherent subtrees and do not map cleanly to resource extensions or sizes.
implication: Control values should be modeled as parser state, not extracted payload metadata.
